label: 7285 Commodity Credit Corporation sales price restrictions

children.heading: General sales authority
children.label: a General sales authority
children.text: The Commodity Credit Corporation may sell any commodity owned or controlled by the Corporation at any price that the Secretary determines will maximize returns to the Corporation.

children.heading: Nonapplication of sales price restrictions
children.label: b Nonapplication of sales price restrictions

children.text: a sale for a new or byproduct use;
a sale of peanuts or oilseeds for the extraction of oil;
a sale for seed or feed if the sale will not substantially impair any loan program;
a sale of a commodity that has substantially deteriorated in quality or as to which there is a danger of loss or waste through deterioration or spoilage;
a sale for the purpose of establishing a claim arising out of a contract or against a person who has committed fraud, misrepresentation, or other wrongful act with respect to the commodity;
a sale for export, as determined by the Corporation; and
a sale for other than a primary use.

children.label: A
children.text: in any area in the United States (including the Virgin Islands) declared by the President to be an acute distress area because of unemployment or other economic cause, if the President finds that the use will not displace or interfere with normal marketing of agricultural commodities; and

children.label: B
children.text: in connection with any major disaster determined by the President to warrant assistance by the Federal Government under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 5121

children.label: A
children.text: the storage of the commodity; and

children.label: B
children.text: the handling and transportation costs in making delivery of the commodity to designated agencies at 1 or more central locations in each State or other area.

children.heading: Efficient operations
children.label: d Efficient operations
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to the sale of a commodity the disposition of which is desirable in the interest of the effective and efficient conduct of the operations of the Corporation because of the small quantity of the commodity involved, or because of the age, location, or questionable continued storability of the commodity.
